heading: Administrative law judges
label: 1305 Administrative law judges
text: For the purpose of sections 3105, 3344, 4301(2)(D), and 5372 of this title and the provisions of that relate to administrative law judges, the Office of Personnel Management may, and for the purpose of , the Merit Systems Protection Board may investigate, prescribe regulations, appoint advisory committees as necessary, recommend legislation, subpena witnesses and records, and pay witness fees as established for the courts of the United States.section 5335(a)(B) of this titlesection 7521 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1966ActDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large (5th sentence).5 U.S.C. 1010(5th sentence), .June 11, 1946, ch. 324, § 1160 Stat. 244Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.
1967ActThis section amends to correct a typographical error.5 U.S.C. 1305

Amendments1998— struck out “require reports by agencies, issue reports, including an annual report to Congress,” after “may investigate,”.Pub. L. 105–3621992— substituted “sections 3105” for “section 3105”.Pub. L. 102–3781978— substituted provisions respecting functions pursuant to specified sections of this title of the Office of Personnel Management and the Merit Systems Protection Board for provisions respecting the functions pursuant to specified sections of this title of the Civil Service Commission.Pub. L. 95–454substituted “Administrative law judges” for “Hearing examiners” in section catchline and “administrative law judges” for “hearing examiners” in text.Pub. L. 95–251

Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by substituting “5372” for “5362” effective on first day of first applicable pay period beginning on or after the 90th day after , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .section 801(a)(3)(B)(iii) of Pub. Termination of Advisory CommitteesAdvisory committees in existence on , excluding committees composed wholly of full-time officers or employees of the Federal Government, to terminate not later than the expiration of the 2-year period following , unless, in the case of a committee established by the President or an officer of the Federal Government, such committee is renewed by appropriate action prior to the expiration of such 2-year period, or in the case of a committee established by the Congress, its duration is otherwise provided for by law. See .Jan. 5, 1973Jan. 5, 1973section 1013 of this title
